items.q: What is the difference between the tundra and the taiga biomes?
items.correct: Tundra is treeless cold biome (too cold and permafrost-dominated for trees), while taiga (boreal forest) is the band of coniferous forest just south of the tundra in subarctic latitudes.
items.misconception: Tundra and taiga are the same biome with different names.
items.random_wrong: Tundra is in the Arctic and taiga is in the Antarctic.
items.why: There is no taiga in the Antarctic because there is no continental land at that latitude. The biomes are distinct (treeless vs forested) — defined by whether the growing season permits trees.

items.q: Why do meanders migrate across a floodplain?
items.correct: Faster water on the outside of the bend causes erosion (river cliff); slower water on the inside causes deposition (slip-off slope) — over time the channel shifts laterally.
items.misconception: Meanders form because rivers flow around obstacles like rocks.
items.random_wrong: Meanders form because the river is going downhill very steeply.
items.why: Meanders form on gentle gradients, not steep ones, and self-organise rather than dodging obstacles. Students project an intuitive 'water goes around things' model onto a fluid dynamic process.

items.correct: Development that meets the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own needs — balancing economic, social and environmental goals.
items.misconception: Sustainable development means stopping all economic growth.
items.random_wrong: Sustainable development means using only solar power.
items.why: Students treat sustainability as anti-development. The Brundtland definition is about balance and intergenerational equity — not zero growth or one specific technology.
The answer examiners want is: Ground frozen for 2+ years. “Permanent snow on hills” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which biome lies south of the tundra?
items.why: Correct answer: Taiga (boreal forest). “Savanna” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Roughly how short is the tundra growing season (days)?
items.why: The real figure is 50. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
